data.title: Specialized skill/agent/prompt authoring sub-agent
data.description:
## Context

Review of 265 bugs across the last week revealed a class of failures not addressed by stage-boundary precondition mechanisms: bugs inside skill files, agent definitions, and prompt templates themselves — positional bias (first example copied when a later one was operative), multi-question Phase 1 turns, double-prefix agent registrations (dso:dso:scope-drift-reviewer), bare ticket-create commands without required flags, and inconsistent template variants in the same file. Examples: 027a-ad30, 7899-a46b, b784-fe6e, 3cb1-429e.

When a general-purpose sub-agent edits a skill file or agent definition, it lacks the domain knowledge to recognize these failure modes. A specialized authoring sub-agent — trained on DSO's skill conventions, prompt-structure expectations, agent-registration rules, and the behavioral-testing-standard — would catch these before commit.

## Success Criteria

- A dedicated sub-agent (dso:skill-author or equivalent) handles creation and modification of files under plugins/dso/skills/, plugins/dso/agents/, and plugins/dso/docs/workflows/prompts/
- The agent enforces DSO-specific authoring conventions: operative-template-first ordering, one-question-per-turn dialogue structure, dso: prefix normalization for agent references, required ticket-create flags, multi-variant template consistency
- Sprint and fix-bug dispatch this agent (not general-purpose) when the task's file impact targets skill/agent/prompt files

## Dependencies

None

## Approach

TBD. Needs /dso:brainstorm before implementation (placeholder epic — scrutiny skipped).
